content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suire 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(9t6) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
December 23,2005
Marie Michel Macias, City Clerk
City of Pomona
City Hall, 505 S. Garey Avenue
Pomona, CA9l769-3186
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A.-05-250
Dear Ms. Macias:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the City of Pomona for
advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ,'Act,).1
QUESTION
May the semi-annual statement for the second half of 2005 be combined with the
semi-annual statement for the first half of 2006 in connection with the January 10,2006,
sPecial election? 
coNCLUSToN
Candidates and committees involved in the January 70,2006, special election
may file a combined statement that will satisfy the requirements to file the semi-annual
statement for the second half of 2005 and the semi-annual statement for the first half of
2006. The combined statement will be filed on July 37,2006, covering the period
December 25,2005, through June 30, 2006. A filing schedule containing the filing
deadlines and periods covered is enclosed with this letter.
FACTS
The City of Pomona is conducting a special election on January l0,2OO6,to fill
the mayoral vacancy.
' Govemment Code sections 81000-91014. Commissionregulations appear attitle2,sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
;i;1tr


Our File No. 4-05-250
page2
ANALYSIS
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two pre-election campaign statements disclosing contributions received
and expenditures made in connection with an election. These pre-election statements are
generally filed 40 days before the election and again 12 d,aysbefore the election.
(Section 84200.8.) semi-annual statementr ure hl"d January 3l and.July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
There is a mechanism in the Act thatpermits the combining of statements when
an oygrlapping reporting period occurs or the filing of a semi-annual statement would be
an additional, unnecessary filing. (Section 84205.) Furthermore, section g20lsO)
provides that the cumulative amount of contributions receive d andexpenditures made in
one year in connection with an election to be held in another year maybe calculated
ending on the closing date of the first semi-annual statement ntea after the election. In
your situation, the closing date for the second pre-election statement is Decemb er 24,
2005, which leaves only seven days for thesemi-annual reporting period ending
December 31,2005. Therefore, we concludethatcandidatls and committees involved in
the January 10,2006, special election may file a combined semi-annual statement, due
July 3 1 , 2006, covering the period December 25 , zoo5 , through June 3 0, 2006.
If I may be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916)
323-693s.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
} .,.7.0-Jdi*, urn f
By: Dixie Howard C<L)
Manager, Filing Officer programs
Enclosure
